style_selection.primary_layout_name: Identity-centric multi-cloud control plane and landing zones

secondary_palette_donor.why: best palette donor for selected primary layout

communication_intent: Central control plane is primary governance anchor
Bands below communicate lifecycle and support services
Right panels communicate governed targets
guardrails: wrap text inside boxes
do not overlap text or connectors
place technology components in semantically correct zones
run spelling and label quality checks after rendering

sustainability: Efficient managed services and right-sized capacity

assumptions: The target environment has access to standard cloud, integration, and observability services.
Security, audit, and operational controls are required for production readiness.
constraints: Keep executive overview diagrams readable and decompose detailed services into companion views.
Use official draw.io icons where available and fallback to styled rounded rectangles for abstract capabilities.
architectural_decisions: Use managed cloud services where they reduce operational burden.
Apply policy-as-code in CI/CD to enforce architectural controls before deployment.
Centralize identity and policy governance while allowing cloud-local execution.
Use separate landing zones to enforce blast-radius control.
architecture_model.communication_goal: Policy-as-Spec Architecture Control Platform connects business capabilities, platform services, integrations, and cross-cutting controls to deliver governed, resilient, and measurable outcomes for CIO stakeholders.

prepared_input.business_outcome_description: Policy-as-Spec Architecture Control Platform connects business capabilities, platform services, integrations, and cross-cutting controls to deliver governed, resilient, and measurable outcomes for CIO stakeholders.

layout_recommendations.user_selection_guidance: Offer recommendation 1 and 2 to the user when interactive selection is possible; otherwise use recommendation 1 as the default.

selected_recommendation.primary_layout_name: Identity-centric multi-cloud control plane and landing zones

secondary_palette_donor.why: best palette donor for selected primary layout

Bands below communicate lifecycle and support services
Right panels communicate governed targets
guardrails: wrap text inside boxes
do not overlap text or connectors

sustainability: Efficient managed services and right-sized capacity

assumptions: The target environment has access to standard cloud, integration, and observability services.
Security, audit, and operational controls are required for production readiness.
constraints: Keep executive overview diagrams readable and decompose detailed services into companion views.
Use official draw.io icons where available and fallback to styled rounded rectangles for abstract capabilities.

enriched_architecture_model.communication_goal: Policy-as-Spec Architecture Control Platform connects business capabilities, platform services, integrations, and cross-cutting controls to deliver governed, resilient, and measurable outcomes for CIO stakeholders.

recommended_views.name: Executive / Business Capability View
recommended_views.layout_strategy: value chain or capability map with key enablers, business outcomes, and a CIO-ready outcome statement

recommended_views.name: Logical / Solution Architecture View
recommended_views.layout_strategy: primary chosen reference layout with central platform boundary, capability groups, actors, systems, and cross-cutting bands
recommended_views.audience: enterprise architects, solution architects, platform owners

recommended_views.name: Technical Services & Infrastructure Detail
recommended_views.layout_strategy: runtime and deployment view grouped by compute, storage, networking, security, CI/CD, resilience, and observability
recommended_views.audience: engineering leads, platform teams, cloud/security teams

recommended_views.name: Integration & Data Flow View
recommended_views.layout_strategy: orthogonal flow view emphasizing APIs, events, sync/async integrations, payload types, and system boundaries
recommended_views.audience: integration architects, data architects, engineering teams

hybrid_layout_guidance: Use central platform plus value-chain flows when the solution has a dominant control plane and clear inputs/outputs.
Add horizontal governance bands when cross-cutting controls are central to the architecture.
Use swimlanes when operational sequence or ownership is the primary message.
Use progression stacks when data or maturity moves through staged zones.

technology_placement_report.title: Policy-as-Spec Architecture Control Platform
technology_placement_report.guidance: Warnings are heuristics. Use them to adjust the normalized JSON before rendering when needed.

working.business_outcome_description: Policy-as-Spec Architecture Control Platform connects business capabilities, platform services, integrations, and cross-cutting controls to deliver governed, resilient, and measurable outcomes for CIO stakeholders.

expansions.title: Architecture Control Plane — Level 2 Expansion
expansions.subtitle: Detailed expansion of Architecture Control Plane

expansions.title: Architecture Assurance Fabric — Level 2 Expansion
expansions.subtitle: Detailed expansion of Architecture Assurance Fabric

orchestration_report.final_output_policy: User-facing outputs should be PNG and .drawio only; evaluation and JSON files are internal/debug only.
orchestration_report.next_step: Feed the generated variant prompt markdown files into drawio-skill to render three PNG and three .drawio outputs.
